back_translation: When the time drew near for David to die, he charged Solomon his son, saying:
“I am about to go the way that all the people of the world must go. Be strong and be a man of valor.

Keep the charge of the LORD your God, walking in his ways, keeping his statutes, his commandments, his ordinances, and his testimonies, as written in the Law of Moses, so that you may prosper in all that you do and wherever you turn,

so that the LORD may fulfill the word he spoke concerning me: ‘If your sons take heed to their way, walking before me faithfully with all their heart and all their soul, you shall never lack a man on the throne of Israel.’

Moreover, you know what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me—what he did to the two commanders of Israel's army, Abner son of Ner and Amasa son of Jether, whom he killed, shedding the blood of war in peacetime, and staining the belt on his waist and the sandals on his feet with the blood of war.
Therefore act according to your wisdom, and do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.
But show steadfast love to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them eat at your table, for they came to help me when I fled from Absalom your brother.

Behold, Shimei son of Gera, the Benjamite from Bahurim, is with you. He cursed me with a bitter curse on the day I went to Mahanaim, but when he came down to meet me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the LORD, ‘I will not put you to death with the sword.’
Now do not hold him guiltless, for you are a wise man. You will know what to do to him, and bring his gray head down to Sheol with blood.”
Then David rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David.
David reigned over Israel forty years; in Hebron he reigned seven years, and in Jerusalem thirty-three years.
So Solomon sat on the throne of David his father, and his kingdom was very firmly established.
Now Adonijah son of Haggith came to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon. She asked, “Do you come in peace?” He answered, “In peace.”
Then he said, “I have something to tell you.” She said, “Speak.”
He said, “You know that the kingdom was mine, and all Israel set their hope on me to reign. But the kingdom has turned about and become my brother's, for it was his from the LORD.
Now I have one request to make of you; do not refuse me.” She said, “Speak.”
He said, “Please speak to King Solomon—for he will not refuse you—that he give me Abishag the Shunammite as my wife.”
Bathsheba said, “Very well; I will speak to the king for you.”

So Bathsheba went to King Solomon to speak to him about Adonijah. The king rose to meet her, bowed to her, then sat on his throne, and had a seat brought for the king's mother, and she sat at his right.
She said, “I have one small request to make of you; do not refuse me.” The king said to her, “Make your request, my mother, for I will not refuse you.”
She said, “Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah your brother as wife.”

King Solomon answered his mother, “And why do you request Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? You might as well request the kingdom for him—for he is my older brother—and for Abiathar the priest and for Joab son of Zeruiah!”
Then King Solomon swore by the LORD, “May God do so to me and more also, if Adonijah has not spoken this word at the cost of his own life!
Now, as the LORD lives—who established me and set me on the throne of David my father, and who made me a dynasty as he promised—surely Adonijah shall be put to death this day.”
So King Solomon commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Benaiah went out and struck Adonijah down, and he died.

As for Abiathar the priest, the king said, “Go to Anathoth, to your fields, for you deserve to die. But today I will not put you to death, because you carried the ark of the Lord GOD before David my father, and because you shared in all my father's affliction.”
So Solomon removed Abiathar from being priest to the LORD, fulfilling the word of the LORD that he had spoken concerning the house of Eli at Shiloh.
When the news reached Joab—for Joab had sided with Adonijah, though he had not sided with Absalom—Joab fled to the tent of the LORD and took hold of the horns of the altar.
It was reported to King Solomon, “Joab has fled to the tent of the LORD, and behold, he is beside the altar.” So Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada, saying, “Go, strike him down.”

Benaiah came to the tent of the LORD and said to Joab, “Thus says the king: Come out!” But he answered, “No, I will die here.” So Benaiah brought the king word, saying, “Thus Joab spoke, and thus he answered me.”
The king said to him, “Do as he says. Strike him down and bury him, so as to remove from me and from the house of my father the innocent blood that Joab shed.

The LORD will return his blood on his own head, because he struck down two men more righteous and better than himself and killed them with the sword, without my father David's knowledge—Abner son of Ner, commander of Israel's army, and Amasa son of Jether, commander of Judah's army.
So their blood shall return on the head of Joab and on the head of his descendants forever; but for David, his descendants, his house, and his throne, there shall be peace from the LORD forever.”
Then Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up, struck Joab down, and killed him, and he was buried at his own house in the wilderness.
And the king set Benaiah son of Jehoiada over the army in his place, and the king set Zadok the priest in the place of Abiathar.
Then the king sent and summoned Shimei and said to him, “Build yourself a house in Jerusalem and live there, and do not go out from there to anywhere.
For on the day you go out and cross the Kidron Valley, know for certain that you shall surely die; your blood shall be on your own head.”
Shimei said to the king, “The word is good. As my lord the king has said, so will your servant do.” So Shimei lived in Jerusalem a long time.
But at the end of three years, two of Shimei's slaves ran away to Achish son of Maacah, king of Gath, and Shimei was told, “Behold, your slaves are in Gath.”
So Shimei rose, saddled his donkey, and went to Gath, to Achish, to seek his slaves; and Shimei went and brought his slaves back from Gath.
It was reported to Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and returned.

So the king sent and summoned Shimei and said to him, “Did I not make you swear by the LORD and warn you, ‘On the day you go out anywhere, know for certain that you shall surely die’? And you answered me, ‘The word I have heard is good.’
Why then have you not kept the oath to the LORD and the command that I gave you?”
The king also said to Shimei, “You know in your heart all the evil that you did to David my father. Therefore the LORD will return your evil on your own head.
But King Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before the LORD forever.”
Then the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and he went out and struck Shimei down, and he died. So the kingdom was firmly established in the hand of Solomon.
